title: prunus padus f. rufoferruginea
intro: Prunus padus f. rufoferruginea, also known as the bird cherry or European bird cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Prunus genus. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunus padus f. rufoferruginea include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of cold and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often made from its flowers, leaves, and bark, and are used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
